response:
THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS. THE RADIUS OF THE 1st CIRCLE IS r, AND EACH SUCCESSIVE CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS. THIS MEANS THE RADIUS OF THE n-th CIRCLE IS r × (1.1)^(n-1). THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR² FOR n = 1 TO 8. THIS CAN BE EXPRESSED AS π[r² + (1.1r)² + (1.1²r)² + ... + (1.1⁷r)²]. THE TOTAL AREA IS πr² × [1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + (1.1)⁶ + (1.1)⁸ + (1.1)¹⁰ + (1.1)¹² + (1.1)¹⁴]. THIS IS THE EXPRESSION FOR THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES.

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πr × (1.1)⁴. THE GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER IS 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE GAP. THIS IS (2πr × (1.1)⁴) / 0.5. THIS IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE.
